news.title:
Credit card spending in FY26 slowed to 11% from 15% in FY25, reaching Rs 23.25 lakh crore due to regulatory clampdowns and UPI shift.
SBI Card reduced new card issuance to 8 lakh/quarter; revolving credit users dropped to 23%, impacting banks' revenue from interest and MDR.
news.summary:
Credit card spending in FY26 slowed to 11% from 15% in FY25, reaching Rs 23.25 lakh crore due to regulatory clampdowns and UPI shift.
SBI Card reduced new card issuance to 8 lakh/quarter; revolving credit users dropped to 23%, impacting banks' revenue from interest and MDR.

news.title:
SBI Cards, Indian Oil, IRFC among stocks turning ex-dividend this week.
Corporate actions include dividends, bonus shares, and stock splits, impacting liquidity and stock prices.
news.summary:
SBI Cards, Indian Oil, IRFC among stocks turning ex-dividend this week.

news.title: SBI Card declares ₹2.50/share dividend, rewarding its shareholders.
news.summary: SBI Card declares ₹2.50/share dividend, rewarding its shareholders.

news.title:
SBI Card MD highlights UPI, credit cards now complementary in digital payments.
Credit cards adapt to UPI tokenisation, enhancing security with codes, biometrics.
news.summary:
SBI Card MD highlights UPI, credit cards now complementary in digital payments.
Credit cards adapt to UPI tokenisation, enhancing security with codes, biometrics.

news.title:
SBI Cards held a one-on-one investor/analyst meet on 02.03.2026 with Matthews India Fund FPI.
Details of the meeting are uploaded on the company's website; public domain information was shared.
news.summary:

news.title:
SBI Cards held one-on-one investor/analyst meetings on 09.02.2026 in Mumbai.
Participants included ICICI Prudential MF, Nippon India MF, Axis MF, Bandhan MF, and Quant MF.
news.summary:
SBI Cards held one-on-one investor/analyst meetings on 09.02.2026 in Mumbai.

news.title:
SBI Cards reported an 8% YoY growth in cards, market share at 18.8%.
YoY spend rose 33%, revenue grew 11%, PAT up 45%, credit cost at 8.3%.
news.summary:
SBI Cards reported an 8% YoY growth in cards, market share at 18.8%.
YoY spend rose 33%, revenue grew 11%, PAT up 45%, credit cost at 8.3%.

news.title:
SBI Cards' Q3 shows profitability growth supported by asset quality and spending trends.
However, cards in force (CIF) and receivables growth remain subdued, dividing analysts' opinions.
news.summary:
SBI Cards' Q3 shows profitability growth supported by asset quality and spending trends.
However, cards in force (CIF) and receivables growth remain subdued, dividing analysts' opinions.

news.title: Morgan Stanley retains Underweight rating on SBI Cards, cuts target price to 665.
news.summary: Morgan Stanley retains Underweight rating on SBI Cards, cuts target price to 665.

news.title:
SBI Cards disclosed the audio recording of its Q3 FY26 earnings call held on Jan 28, 2026.
The recording is available on the SBI Card website, fulfilling SEBI Regulation 30 compliance.
news.summary:

news.title:
L&T posted mixed Q3 results with net profit at ₹3,215 crore, down 4.3% YoY; revenue rose 10.5% YoY but missed estimates.
SBI Cards saw net profit rise 45.3% YoY to ₹556.6 crore, with total spends up 33% YoY; M&M Fin and Balkrishna posted mixed results.
news.summary:
L&T posted mixed Q3 results with net profit at ₹3,215 crore, down 4.3% YoY; revenue rose 10.5% YoY but missed estimates.
SBI Cards saw net profit rise 45.3% YoY to ₹556.6 crore, with total spends up 33% YoY; M&M Fin and Balkrishna posted mixed results.

news.title:
SBI Cards reports Q3 9M revenue of ₹15,520 cr and net profit of ₹1,557 cr.
Gross NPA at 2.9%, Net NPA at 1.3%; ₹12 cr impact from new labor codes.
news.summary:
SBI Cards reports Q3 9M revenue of ₹15,520 cr and net profit of ₹1,557 cr.
Gross NPA at 2.9%, Net NPA at 1.3%; ₹12 cr impact from new labor codes.

news.title:
SBI Cards' board meeting on Jan 28, 2026, to approve Q3 and 9-month FY25 results.
Trading window closed since Jan 1, 2026, opens 48 hours post results announcement.
news.summary:
SBI Cards' board meeting on Jan 28, 2026, to approve Q3 and 9-month FY25 results.

news.title:
Macquarie upgraded SBI, Paytm, and life insurers while downgrading Bandhan Bank, SBI Cards.
SBI's target raised to ₹1,150 (+15%), Paytm to ₹1,265 (-2%), and SBI Life to ₹2,360 (+14%).
news.summary:
Macquarie upgraded SBI, Paytm, and life insurers while downgrading Bandhan Bank, SBI Cards.
SBI's target raised to ₹1,150 (+15%), Paytm to ₹1,265 (-2%), and SBI Life to ₹2,360 (+14%).

news.title:
NBFCs show recovery post-regulatory tightening, with diversified players like Bajaj Finance, L&T Finance, and Shriram Finance positioned for growth.
Muthoot Finance benefits from strong fundamentals, while SBI Cards expects ROA improvement to 4.5% by FY28 due to easing credit costs.
news.summary:
NBFCs show recovery post-regulatory tightening, with diversified players like Bajaj Finance, L&T Finance, and Shriram Finance positioned for growth.
Muthoot Finance benefits from strong fundamentals, while SBI Cards expects ROA improvement to 4.5% by FY28 due to easing credit costs.
